title: limonium ithacense
intro: Limonium ithacense, also known as Cretan sea lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the island of Crete in Greece. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's medicinal properties have been found to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Limonium ithacense can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
